# Working at TPL

## Culture & Values

### Kindness, transparency, clarity of expectations, and hard work
- We expect that everyone treats one another with kindness and respect
- We address conflict directly and constructively
- We hold ourselves accountable to making decisions transparently
- We celebrate one another's successes and support one another through our missteps
- We combat the hidden curriculum of academia — if anything is unclear, please let us know

### Managing up
- Take responsibility for understanding how your work fits into the bigger picture
- Track action items and next steps in every meeting
- Clarify who is doing what and by when whenever a to-do comes up
- Volunteer to take notes, facilitate, and take on tasks when you have capacity
- Kindly remind others of commitments when deadlines are approaching

### Communicating clearly, early, and often
- If you feel overburdened or don't have enough to do — tell your manager
- If you're worried about missing a deadline — speak up early
- Internal messages should generally be responded to within 2 hours during normal hours
- Review all written communication carefully before sending

### Following through on our commitments
- Do what you say you will do, by when you say you will do it
- We expect everyone to accomplish work reflecting a full and focused 40-45 hours per week on average
- Check in with your manager when your workload feels unbalanced

### Being useful and responsive to our partners
- Reply to partner emails within 48 hours
- Track and follow up on all partner meeting to-dos by the stated deadline
- The most junior person on a call is responsible for taking notes and tracking next steps
- Be prepared, on time, and fully present in all partner meetings

## Communication Tools

- **Slack** — primary tool for internal communication. Keep your status up to date.
- **Email** — primary tool for external communication
- **Zoom** — used for most hybrid and remote meetings

## Working Hours

We encourage you to set up regular working hours with your manager. We expect all full-time team members to be available between 9am and 5pm in their time zone unless otherwise discussed.

Receiving a Slack message or email after hours is not an invitation to work after hours. When in doubt, ask if something is urgent.

## Working Remotely

HKS policy is that all full-time staff work in-person from Harvard three days per week. Cambridge-based staff are expected to adhere to this policy.

Staff working from home more than two days per week need a formal Flex Arrangement approved by the school, arranged with your manager and processed by Noe.

## Vacation & Out of Office

Give your manager as much notice as possible when you plan to be out — ideally a month in advance, but at minimum two weeks. Vacation, holiday, and sick time must be entered and approved in PeopleSoft.

Set up an out of office message for all planned time away, including campus holidays.

### Fellows Leave Policy
Fellows should use the [PTO Tracker](link) to record time off in coordination with their manager.

- [2025 Fellow Leave Policy](link)
- [2025 Fellow PTO Tracker Template](link)

## Calendar Management

- **Share your calendar** with all TPL
